def can_split_array(nums, m):
    n = len(nums)
    if n == 1:
        return nums[0] >= m

    # We'll use dynamic programming to solve this problem
    # dp[i][j] represents whether the subarray nums[i..j] can be split into (j-i+1) arrays
    dp = [[False] * n for _ in range(n)]

    # Base case: single element subarrays are always valid
    for i in range(n):
        dp[i][i] = True

    # Fill the dp table for subarrays of length 2 to n
    for length in range(2, n + 1):
        for i in range(n - length + 1):
            j = i + length - 1
